steps.title: Convert radicals to exponential form
steps.content:
Rewrite the numerator and denominator using fractional exponents:
$$\sqrt{x^7} = x^{7/2}$$
$$\sqrt[3]{x^4} = x^{4/3}$$
The fraction becomes:
$$\frac{x^{7/2}}{x^{4/3}}$$

steps.title: Apply the quotient rule for exponents
steps.content:
When dividing powers with the same base, subtract the exponents:
$$x^{7/2} \div x^{4/3} = x^{7/2 - 4/3}$$
To subtract the exponents, use a common denominator of 6:
$$\frac{7}{2} - \frac{4}{3} = \frac{21}{6} - \frac{8}{6} = \frac{13}{6}$$
